pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 1.8 to 17 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 8, failing to leverage Java 17 features and potentially causing compatibility issues with libraries requiring Java 17.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot version from 2.7.4 to 3.3.13 to align with Java 17 and Jakarta EE namespace changes.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 17 as a baseline and uses javax.* namespaces; Spring Boot 3.x requires Java 17+ and uses jakarta.* namespaces. Mixing them causes runtime classpath errors.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Cloud version from 2021.0.4 to 2023.0.6 to match the Spring Boot 3.x release train.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Cloud 2021.x is incompatible with Spring Boot 3.x due to API changes and namespace migrations (javax to jakarta).

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven Compiler Plugin version to 3.15.1-SNAPSHOT.
Remove exclusion of junit-vintage-engine from spring-boot-starter-test.
Remove explicit dependencies for javax.xml.bind jaxb-api, jaxb-impl, and jaxb-core.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x and Java 17+ environments typically manage JAXB dependencies differently or require Jakarta XML Binding. The old javax dependencies are removed to avoid conflicts or because they are no longer needed/compatible in the new stack.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add <release> configuration to maven-compiler-plugin in the main build section.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using <source> and <target> is deprecated in newer Java versions. <release> is the preferred way to set the Java version for compilation and ensures compatibility with the target JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace <source> and <target> with <release> in the maven-compiler-plugin configuration.
pom.xml.why_exists: The <source> and <target> tags are deprecated for Java 9+. Using <release> is required for proper compilation against Java 17+ and ensures the correct module path and API usage.
